name: Character Count Map
summary: Tally every character of s in a hash map, then subtract each character of t; any missing key or negative count means it is not an anagram.
visualizationIntent: init: show an empty character-count map. count phase: scan s left to right, adding one to the current character's tally so the map fills up and each touched key flashes. verify phase: scan t left to right, decrementing the matching key; if a key is missing or its count would drop below zero, freeze on that character as an error. return: if every tally settles back to zero the strings are anagrams.

lineExplanations.3: If the two strings have different lengths they cannot be anagrams.
lineExplanations.4: Return false immediately when the lengths differ.
lineExplanations.5: Create an empty map that will store how many times each character appears in s.
lineExplanations.6: Walk through every index of the first string s.
lineExplanations.7: Read the current character of s.
lineExplanations.8: Add one to this character's running tally in the map.
lineExplanations.9: Walk through every index of the second string t.
lineExplanations.10: Read the current character of t.
lineExplanations.11: Check whether this character was ever counted from s.
lineExplanations.12: Return false because t contains a character that s does not have.
lineExplanations.13: Cancel one occurrence of this character by decrementing its tally.
lineExplanations.14: Check whether the tally has gone below zero.
lineExplanations.15: Return false because t uses this character more often than s does.
lineExplanations.16: Every tally cancelled out cleanly, so t is an anagram of s.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: defines the class wrapper expected by the platform.
syntaxExplanations.2: def isAnagram(self, s, t): defines a method that receives the two strings s and t.
syntaxExplanations.3: if len(s) != len(t): compares the lengths of the two strings.
syntaxExplanations.4: return False sends back the boolean False and stops the method.
syntaxExplanations.5: counts = {} creates an empty Python dictionary.
syntaxExplanations.6: for i in range(len(s)): loops i over every valid index of s.
syntaxExplanations.7: ch = s[i] reads the character of s at index i.
syntaxExplanations.8: counts[ch] = counts.get(ch, 0) + 1 reads the current count (0 if absent) and stores one more.
syntaxExplanations.9: for j in range(len(t)): loops j over every valid index of t.
syntaxExplanations.10: d = t[j] reads the character of t at index j.
syntaxExplanations.11: if d not in counts: checks whether the dictionary has no key d.
syntaxExplanations.12: return False sends back False and stops the method.
syntaxExplanations.13: counts[d] = counts[d] - 1 stores the tally reduced by one.
syntaxExplanations.14: if counts[d] < 0: checks whether the stored tally is now negative.
syntaxExplanations.15: return False sends back False and stops the method.
syntaxExplanations.16: return True sends back True to report a valid anagram.
